comments.content:
Hello!

I've been practicing the Sphere of Protection daily for a bit over a month now (I'm up to the invocation of Spirit Above), and I was hoping to get some clarification on a couple of things.

1. I don't normally have frequent nightmares, but within the last month or so, I've noticed a distinct increase in anxiety-inducing, genuinely upsetting dreams. Could this be linked to my practice of the SoP? It's the only significant change to my life (aside from the new year) that I can think of.

2. I find the associated visualizations of some elements much more difficult than others. The one I have for Water came so easily and clearly to me that I was actually startled by it. Air is a bit more challenging, but still very clear. Earth is middling, and Fire is very difficult. I've yet to land on a visualization for Fire that feels right. Is this normal?

3. A little while ago, you mentioned the concept of tracing one of the elemental symbols from the SoP over a glass of water before drinking it to promote health. Does it matter which symbol you use? Is there a verbal component to this sort of ritual?

Thanks, and welcome back!

replies.title: Re: SoP queries
replies.content:
1) Yes. You may be clearing old business away from your subconscious.

2) It's perfectly normal. Over time things will balance out.

3) Since it's a glass of water, you use the banishing water symbol. Then you put your right hand over the glass, and imagine a stream of golden energy flowing down from your hand to charge the water. Imagine it filling the water with energies of health and vitality. Then drink the water. No words are involved, so long as you do the whole thing.

comments.title: Huzza
comments.content:
Archdruid,

Welcome back from your much deserved break, I'm happy your month off was so productive! I look forward to another year of your guidance through the forest of our reality.

During the holidays I got into a conversation with my sister-in-law about Gestalt education, and how its affected our entire cultures outlook on the world. Everything is broken down into its smallest component and becomes a mechanical part of all other things.

I came to realize that the Gestalt education system is pretty exactly derived from the old debate between Plato and Aristotle. With the latter laying out the foundation for the modern academy, while the former laid the foundation for modern occultism. Am I right in thinking this?

I realized how difficult it is dealing with complex systems ecology, human society, and etc...by breaking everything down to its smallest component. It becomes a barrier to clear thinking because most ecological systems aren't uni-causal. There are so many interconnected parts in motion during any given event that tracing down a single point of causation isn't just pointless, it's counter-productive.

Magic seems to work both sides, we break down a concept during discursive meditation, then try to find a way to work with the "true form" of the concept on the higher planes using symbols and ritual.

Not surprisingly I've found that mediating on works like the cosmic doctrine is allowing me to figure out a way to explain complex systems like the human ecosystem in was previously unable to. I've restarted work on my PhD (unofficially) and I'm noticing concepts like the three elements from the Druid system, the paths in space from the cosmic doctrine, and etc...are perfect metaphors for many of the complexities I previously observed.

I've also realized that the university system keeps meddling with occult systems, but never quite getting the results they expect. They break down concepts and try to rearrange them, but can never seem to move them from the mental plane to the material. Do you see this too or am I just imagining things?

Regards,

Syfen

replies.title: Re: Huzza
replies.content: Dead on target. Aristotle dissects and leaves the pieces lying about to rot, Plato opens it up and then puts it back together as a living whole. As for the university system, they're in the position of a Dr. Frankenstein who's trying to bring his monster to life but has convinced himself that life doesn't exist. So he wonders why the thing just lies there...
